content.problemStatement:
You are given a matrix AAA of size N√óNN\times NN√óN where each element of the matrix is either OOO or XXX.

An unclaimed territory in the matrix is defined as a set of MMM coordinates (Xi,Yi)(X_i, Y_i)(Xi‚Äč,Yi‚Äč) such that:


M‚Č•1M \ge 1M‚Č•1;
1‚Č§Xi,Yi‚Č§N1 \le X_i, Y_i \le N1‚Č§Xi‚Äč,Yi‚Äč‚Č§N;
(Xi,Yi)‚Č†(Xj,Yj)(X_i, Y_i) \neq (X_j, Y_j)(Xi‚Äč,Yi‚Äč)ÓÄ†=(Xj‚Äč,Yj‚Äč) if (i‚Č†j)(i\neq j)(iÓÄ†=j), that is, no two coordinates are equal;
A(Xi,Yi)=OA_{(X_i, Y_i)} = OA(Xi‚Äč,Yi‚Äč)‚Äč=O for all (1‚Č§i‚Č§M)(1\le i \le M)(1‚Č§i‚Č§M);
A coordinate (Xi,Yi)(X_i, Y_i)(Xi‚Äč,Yi‚Äč) exists in the set iff there is a coordinate (Xj,Yj)(X_j, Y_j)(Xj‚Äč,Yj‚Äč) present in the set such that (Xi,Yi)(X_i, Y_i)(Xi‚Äč,Yi‚Äč) and (Xj,Yj)(X_j, Y_j)(Xj‚Äč,Yj‚Äč) are neighbours. Mathematically, **one** of the following is true:

‚ą£Xi‚ąíXj‚ą£=1|X_i - X_j| = 1‚ą£Xi‚Äč‚ąíXj‚Äč‚ą£=1 and ‚ą£Yi‚ąíYj‚ą£=0|Y_i - Y_j| = 0‚ą£Yi‚Äč‚ąíYj‚Äč‚ą£=0
‚ą£Xi‚ąíXj‚ą£=0|X_i - X_j| = 0‚ą£Xi‚Äč‚ąíXj‚Äč‚ą£=0 and ‚ą£Yi‚ąíYj‚ą£=1|Y_i - Y_j| = 1‚ą£Yi‚Äč‚ąíYj‚Äč‚ą£=1


An unclaimed territory is captured if **no** coordinate of the territory lies on the boundary of the matrix.

Once an unclaimed territory is captured, all its elements change from OOO to XXX.

Print the final matrix after all the possible captures are done.

Note that, a matrix of size N√óNN\times NN√óN has four boundaries, that is, the first row, the first column, the last row, and the last column.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN ‚ÄĒ the size of the row.
The next NNN lines describe the matrix. The ithi^{th}ith of these NNN lines contains a string of length NNN consisting of OOO and XXX.




Output Format
For each test case, output NNN lines. The ithi^{th}ith of these NNN lines contains a string of length NNN consisting of OOO and XXX representing the final matrix.
constraints: A(i,j)A_{(i,j)}A(i,j)‚Äč is either OOO or XXX.
The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 200020002000.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: There is only one unclaimed territory:

{(2,2)}\{(2,2)\}{(2,2)}: This is captured as there is only element which does not lie on the boundary of the matrix.

Test case 222: There are 555 unclaimed territories, out of which, only one does not lie on the boundary of the matrix.
Test case 333: There is only one unclaimed territory:

{(2,2),(2,3)}\{(2,2), (2, 3)\}{(2,2),(2,3)}: This cannot be captured as the coordinate (2,3)(2,3)(2,3) lies on the boundary of the matrix.

Test case 444: There are 333 unclaimed territories, out of which, only one does not lie on the boundary of the matrix.
Thus, only the unclaimed territory {(2,2),(2,3),(3,3)}\{(2, 2), (2,3), (3,3)\}{(2,2),(2,3),(3,3)} would be captured.
